perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized use, dissemination or publication of the Confidential Information as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using a minimal degree of care, but no less than ordinary care, to prevent the unauthorized use, dissemination or publication of the Confidential Information as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care' to 'minimal degree of care, but no less than ordinary care' introduces a contradiction. While 'reasonable' implies a thoughtful and appropriate level of protection, 'minimal' suggests the lowest acceptable standard. This creates uncertainty about the expected level of protection for Confidential Information. This contradicts section 2. Confidential Information. Information that is to be treated as Confidential under this Agreement shall...
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information that is to be treated as Confidential under this Agreement shall A. (i) be disclosed in tangible form (including electronic form) and marked by the Disclosing Party as “Confidential,” “Proprietary” or other appropriate legend indicating the confidential nature of the information or (ii) be disclosed orally or visually and be identified by the Disclosing Party as confidential and then summarized in tangible form, marked in accordance with Section (i) above, and delivered to by the Receiving Party within thirty days after the date of first disclosure. B. include (i) samples and prototypes and (ii) information, in any form or medium, regarding pricing, customers and prospective customers, vendors and vendor lists, costed bills of materials, processes (including but not limited to manufacturing processes), know-how, designs (including but not limited to designs of enclosures and printed circuit boards), formulae, computer programs, databases, methods of operation, sales techniques, business methods or plans, marketing plans and strategies, finances, management, plant and equipment, or any other business information relating to the Disclosing Party, whether constituting a trade secret, proprietary information or otherwise, which has value to the Disclosing Party and is treated by the Disclosing Party as being confidential. C. Whether stated or not, Confidential Information will also include any item, information, document(s) that either party should reasonably expect should remain confidential in their own course of business if that information was their ownership.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care' to 'minimal degree of care, but no less than ordinary care' directly contradicts the expectation of confidentiality established in the 'Contradicted Text'. Section C of the contradicted text expects items to be held to standards that the party should reasonably expect to remain confidential in the course of business, but the change permits minimal care, potentially undermining the confidential nature of the information as defined. This creates uncertainty and inconsistency, thus a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party (i) shall limit access to all Confidential Information to its employees, agents, representatives, consultants and contractors who shall reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above and to third party vendors for the purpose of obtaining price quotations, (ii) shall use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the Purpose, and (iii) shall use due diligence to insure that all such persons are aware of, understand their obligations under, and comply with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party (i) may allow access to all Confidential Information to any employee, agent, representative, consultant, or contractor and to third party vendors, regardless of need for the purpose set forth above, (ii) may use the Confidential Information in connection with any purpose, and (iii) shall not be required to insure that all such persons are aware of, understand their obligations under, and comply with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall limit access' to 'may allow access', 'shall use...solely in connection with the Purpose' to 'may use...in connection with any purpose', and removing the requirement for due diligence in ensuring compliance, this creates a contradiction with the core principle of confidentiality established throughout the agreement. The original text strictly controls access and use, while the modified text grants almost unlimited freedom. This directly contradicts the 'Duty to Protect' stated at the beginning of the original section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty to Protect/Publicity. The Receiving Party shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized use, dissemination or publication of the Confidential Information as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. The Receiving Party (i) shall limit access to all Confidential Information to its employees, agents, representatives, consultants and contractors who shall reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above and to third party vendors for the purpose of obtaining price quotations, (ii) shall use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the Purpose, and (iii) shall use due diligence to insure that all such persons are aware of, understand their obligations under, and comply with the provisions of this Agreement. Each party agrees not to publicize or disclose the existence or terms of this Agreement to any third party without the prior consent of the other party except as required by law (in which case, the party seeking to disclose the information shall give reasonable notice to the other party of its intent to make such a disclosure). Neither party shall make any press release or similar public statement without the prior consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations. Originally, the Receiving Party 'shall limit access' and 'shall use...solely in connection with the Purpose.' The modified text states the Receiving Party 'may allow access' and 'may use...in connection with any purpose.' Furthermore, the original text required due diligence to ensure compliance, which is removed in the changed text. This directly contradicts the 'Duty to Protect' clause in the contradicted text, which requires the Receiving Party to protect Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care and limits access and use. The change from 'shall' to 'may' and the removal of the due diligence requirement create a strong and direct conflict, making the confidentiality obligations unenforceable and creating a contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Disclosing Party and the Disclosing Party’s business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the Disclosing Party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, the Receiving Party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Disclosing Party may be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that its obligations hereunder are suggestions and may not be necessary nor reasonable in order to protect the Disclosing Party and the Disclosing Party’s business, and does not agree that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the Disclosing Party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, the Receiving Party does not agree and acknowledge that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Disclosing Party may not be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation directly contradicts the original understanding of the agreement. By stating the obligations are merely 'suggestions' and denying that monetary damages are inadequate, it undermines the enforceability of the entire NDA. The contradiction creates a strong uncertainty about whether a breach can be adequately addressed or prevented. Section 11. Remedies. The Receiving Party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Disclosing Party and the Disclosing Party’s business.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. The Receiving Party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Disclosing Party and the Disclosing Party’s business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the Disclosing Party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, the Receiving Party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Disclosing Party may be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, and also contradicts the original text. The original and contradicted text state that the obligations are 'necessary and reasonable' and that monetary damages may be inadequate, while the changed text states the obligations are 'suggestions' and denies that monetary damages are inadequate. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, undermining the enforceability of the agreement, falling under the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) days after any written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return all copies of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Within one-hundred (100) days after any written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party may, at its convenience, return some or none of the copies of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'ten (10) days' to 'one-hundred (100) days', 'shall promptly return all' to 'may, at its convenience, return some or none' introduces a significant contradiction. It undermines the Disclosing Party's control over their Confidential Information and weakens the Receiving Party's obligation to return it. The original expectation was a prompt and complete return, ensuring no residual risk of misuse. The modified version allows for a delayed, partial, or even non-existent return, creating a clear inconsistency with the purpose of the NDA and specifically contradicts the intent outlined in Section 1 Purpose of Disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Purpose of Disclosure. The purpose of this Agreement is (i) to permit the parties to evaluate whether to enter into a business relationship and related projects (the “Purpose”) and (ii) in the event such a relationship is commenced, to permit the parties to exchange Confidential Information (as defined below). In connection with the Purpose, either party (the “Disclosing Party”) may disclose Confidential Information (as defined below) to the other party (the “Receiving Party”).
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a prompt and complete return of confidential information within ten days, whereas the changed text allows for a delayed, partial, or even non-existent return at the Receiving Party's convenience. This directly contradicts the 'Purpose of Disclosure' outlined in the contradicted text, which aims to permit the exchange of Confidential Information under the agreement. By weakening the obligation to return the information, the modified text undermines the Disclosing Party's control and increases the risk of misuse, creating a clear conflict with the intended purpose of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that the state courts of Collin or Dallas Counties, Texas and the federal courts located in the Eastern District of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that any court anywhere in the world shall have jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the jurisdiction and venue from specific courts in Texas to 'any court anywhere in the world' creates a massive contradiction. The original clause provides predictability and control over legal disputes, while the modified clause introduces complete uncertainty and potentially exposes the parties to unfavorable legal systems. It undermines the party expectation that was established in section 10. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Texas, without regard to its conflicts of law principles.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Texas, without regard to its conflicts of law principles. The parties agree that the state courts of Collin or Dallas Counties, Texas and the federal courts located in the Eastern District of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, which broadens the jurisdiction to 'any court anywhere in the world,' directly contradicts the contradicted text that specifies exclusive jurisdiction in Texas courts (Collin or Dallas Counties, Texas and the federal courts located in the Eastern District of Texas). This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it introduces complete uncertainty and undermines the original agreement on governing law, which is Texas law, as defined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties, and it is intended to be final expression of their Agreement. No promise, representation, warranty or covenant not included in this document has been or is relied upon by any party. Each party has relied upon its own examination of the warranties, representations and covenants expressly contained in the Agreement itself. No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be of any force unless in writing executed by all parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This document contains some of the Agreement between the parties, and it is not intended to be final expression of their Agreement. Promises, representations, warranties and covenants not included in this document may be relied upon by any party. No party has relied upon its own examination of the warranties, representations and covenants expressly contained in the Agreement itself. Any verbal modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be of full force, even without written execution by all parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely negates the 'Entire Agreement' clause. By stating the document is not the final expression, that external promises can be relied upon, and that verbal modifications are valid, it destroys the purpose of having a written contract. Section 14. Entire Agreement. This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties, and it is intended to be final expression of their Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement. This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties, and it is intended to be final expression of their Agreement. No promise, representation, warranty or covenant not included in this document has been or is relied upon by any party. Each party has relied upon its own examination of the warranties, representations and covenants expressly contained in the Agreement itself. No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be of any force unless in writing executed by all parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' and the original text. The original and contradicted text states that the document contains the complete agreement, while the changed text states it contains 'some' of the agreement and is 'not' the final expression. The original and contradicted text prohibits reliance on external promises; the changed text allows it. The original and contradicted text requires written amendments; the changed text validates verbal modifications. This creates a strong in-text contradiction per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.
